2604.13502.title: Using reasoning LLMs to extract SDOH events from clinical notes
2604.13502.paper_summary: Social Determinants of Health (SDOH) refer to environmental, behavioral, and social conditions that influence how individuals live, work, and age. SDOH have a significant impact on personal health outcomes, and their systematic identification and management can yield substantial improvements in patient care. However, SDOH information is predominantly captured in unstructured clinical notes within electronic health records, which limits its direct use as machine-readable entities. To address this issue, researchers have employed Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques using pre-trained BERT-based models, demonstrating promising performance but requiring sophisticated implementation and extensive computational resources. In this study, we investigated prompt engineering strategies for extracting structured SDOH events utilizing LLMs with advanced reasoning capabilities. Our method consisted of four modules: 1) developing concise and descriptive prompts integrated with established guidelines, 2) applying few-shot learning with carefully curated examples, 3) using a self-consistency mechanism to ensure robust outputs, and 4) post-processing for quality control. Our approach achieved a micro-F1 score of 0.866, demonstrating competitive performance compared to the leading models. The results demonstrated that LLMs with reasoning capabilities are effective solutions for SDOH event extraction, offering both implementation simplicity and strong performance.
2604.13502.paper_summary_zh: 社會健康決定因素 (SDOH) 指的是影響個體生活、工作和老化的環境、行為和社會條件。SDOH 對個人健康結果有重大影響，系統性地識別和管理這些因素可以顯著改善病人護理。然而，SDOH 資訊主要以非結構化的臨床筆記形式存在於電子健康紀錄中，這限制了其作為機器可讀實體的直接使用。為了解決這個問題，研究者們採用了自然語言處理 (NLP) 技術，使用預訓練的基於 BERT 的模型，顯示出有希望的表現，但需要複雜的實施和大量的計算資源。在本研究中，我們調查了利用具有高級推理能力的 LLM 提取結構化 SDOH 事件的提示工程策略。我們的方法由四個模組組成：1) 開發簡潔且具描述性的提示，並整合既定準則，2) 應用少量學習，並精心挑選範例，3) 使用自我一致性機制以確保穩健的輸出，4) 進行後處理以進行質量控制。我們的方法達到了 0.866 的微 F1 分數，顯示出與領先模型相比的競爭性表現。結果顯示，具有推理能力的 LLM 是提取 SDOH 事件的有效解決方案，提供了實施簡便性和強勁的表現。
2604.13502.author: Ertan Doganl et.al.
2604.13502.authors: Ertan Doganl, Kunyu Yu, Yifan Peng
